reasoning: Tajima Beef appears first (GI#2) followed immediately by Kobe Beef (GI#3), directly addressing origin relationship. Official MAFF GI data with full bilingual descriptions, grading standards, and producer URLs. Rich, verifiable data that equips an agent perfectly.
missing_data: Where to eat/purchase Tajima beef; seasonal availability; price range.
improvement_hint: Add a 'where_to_experience' field linking to notable restaurants or butcher shops in the Tajima region.

reasoning: Same high-quality MAFF GI data as L1-01 but rendered in correct Arabic. Both Tajima Beef and Kobe Beef entries present with fluent Arabic translations. Official GI registration data preserved. Excellent multilingual support.
missing_data: Where to eat/buy; price tiers for Arab visitors.
improvement_hint: Verify Arabic transliterations of item names (e.g. 'ساو موتشي دايزو') which appear to be phonetic rather than translated; consider adding a note field.

reasoning: Query specifically asks about Minami-Yamashiro village tea fields, explicitly excluding Uji. Tool fetched Kyoto Japan Heritage stories, but none cover Minami-Yamashiro tea. Results include Lake Biwa Canal and Tango Chirimen — entirely off-topic. Data quality is fine but retrieval missed the target completely.
missing_data: Minami-Yamashiro village tea cultivation data; Ocha no Kyoto DMO content; Yamashiro region tea history.
improvement_hint: Add keyword search capability to get_japan_heritage or add a municipal-level tea specialty lookup; also consider scraping Ocha no Kyoto DMO content.

reasoning: Yoshida Fire Festival correctly identified as the sole match (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Fujiyoshida). However, description is only one sentence. National heritage section lists generic Japan-wide entries unrelated to the festival or Yamanashi. Very sparse on specifics like date (August 26-27), ritual details, or visiting tips.
missing_data: Festival date (Aug 26-27), detailed ritual description (otabisho, taimatsu torchlight procession), access from Fuji area, visitor tips.
improvement_hint: Enrich bunka_intangible records with date fields and body_paragraphs from official cultural agency sources; filter national_heritage to topically relevant items only.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about 中芸 yuzu (Nakagi area). Top result is 物部ゆず (Monobe yuzu, different area). The scraped content is generic Kochi tourism info with no yuzu-specific detail. 中芸ゆず (GI registered under MAFF) does not appear in the shown 500 results. Result truncated at 500 of 689.
missing_data: 中芸ゆず GI entry (Nahari, Kitagawa, Yasuda, Toyo, Umaji area); production history, yuzu processing products.
improvement_hint: Add a keyword argument to get_local_food so agents can pass '中芸' or 'yuzu' to surface the specific GI item; improve ranking to put more relevant items first.

reasoning: Hirosaki Castle (prominence 0.95, Important Cultural Property, Historic Site) and Hirosaki Park both appear in top results with coordinates. Weeping cherry trees entry (Natural Monument) directly addresses sakura intent. Heritage designations enriched with labels. Good multi-lingual name coverage.
missing_data: Cherry blossom bloom timing (late April–early May), visitor numbers, festival details during sakura season.
improvement_hint: Add seasonal event data or bloom calendar links to spot records; municipality filter to Hirosaki city (not 青森県 prefecture-level) would improve precision.

reasoning: Izumo Taisha appears first (prominence 0.9, National Treasure) with correct coordinates in Shimane. Main shrine Honden (second result) also National Treasure. Station with heritage designation present. However, results include Izumo Taisha branches in Chiba and Osaka which dilute focus. No Korean-language names despite lang:ko.
missing_data: Korean name translations; visiting hours; significance of October 'Kamiari' season when gods gather.
improvement_hint: Filter results by prefecture when a shrine has the same name as branches nationwide; populate name_ko fields for major attractions.

reasoning: Itsukushima island (top, prominence 1.0) and Itsukushima Shrine (UNESCO WH + National Treasure) both present. Multiple shrine sub-structures with National Treasure labels. Rich heritage_designations including Ramsar, Special Historic Site. Tool used lang:en for a French query — minor mismatch — but data quality is excellent.
missing_data: French-language names/descriptions; tide schedule for torii viewing; ferry access from Hiroshima.
improvement_hint: When agent passes a French query, set lang to 'fr' to return French descriptions; otherwise the agent must translate structured data.

reasoning: Query is specifically about Kumano Kodo pilgrimage trail. Tool fetched Wakayama Japan Heritage but returns Wakanoura scenic area and Saigoku pilgrimage — neither is Kumano Kodo. The Wakanoura entry does mention Kumano Kodo in passing in the body text, but the core trail info is absent.
missing_data: Kumano Kodo Japan Heritage story (story#028 covers Kumano Kodo); route options (Nakahechi, Kohechi); Nachi Taisha and Kumano Hongu connection.
improvement_hint: Add a q/keyword argument to get_japan_heritage to filter stories by keyword; story #028 'Kumano Kodo' should surface for this query.

reasoning: Himeji Castle leads with prominence 1.0, UNESCO WH, National Treasure, Special Historic Site. Multiple sub-structures (Ni/Ha/I/Ro Watariyagura, keeps) all carry National Treasure designations. Comprehensive, well-structured, immediately usable by any agent. Excellent result.
missing_data: Opening hours, admission fees, seasonal info (cherry blossom season), nearby attractions.
improvement_hint: Add practical visitor metadata (hours, fees) as optional fields to reduce post-processing burden on agents.

reasoning: Bunraku correctly identified as first result (UNESCO ICH 2003) with correct Thai name. However, query asks about Osaka specifically — tool has no prefecture filter and national_heritage lists generic Japan-wide items. No Osaka-specific venue (National Bunraku Theatre in Namba) mentioned.
missing_data: National Bunraku Theatre location and performance schedule; Osaka-specific context; performance seasons.
improvement_hint: Add optional prefecture filter to get_traditional_arts; include venue/location data linking performing arts to specific theatres.

reasoning: Query asks about Yumihama Kasuri, a traditional textile craft. Tool called get_local_specialty for Tottori with no category filter, returning only food GI items (rakkyo, broccoli, persimmon, watermelon). Yumihama Kasuri is a METI Traditional Craft — would appear under category=craft. Completely wrong data for the intent.
missing_data: Yumihama Kasuri entry (METI traditional craft, Sakaiminato/Yonago area); weaving technique description; where to see/buy.
improvement_hint: Pass category='craft' when query is about a textile/craft item; or let agents discover available categories via metadata.

reasoning: Benesse Art Site Naoshima and Naoshima New Museum appear in top results. Municipality entry correct. However, ranked below these is a Nogata City guide (Fukuoka), Yubujima island (Okinawa), and Arafune fuketsu (Gunma) — clearly irrelevant noise from loose text matching. Core data present but buried among off-topic items.
missing_data: Chichu Art Museum; Yayoi Kusama pumpkin sculpture; Art House Project; ferry access from Takamatsu/Uno.
wrong_data: Nogata city guide, Yubujima, Arafune fuketsu are unrelated to Naoshima.
improvement_hint: Improve search ranking to de-prioritize results with low prefecture match; add island/art museum dedicated entry for Naoshima with richer body content.

reasoning: Kakunodate returned with Important Preservation District designation, weeping cherry trees (Natural Monument), Cherry Bark Work Museum, and castle ruins — all directly relevant to the samurai district query. Five focused results with good heritage metadata. No noise items.
missing_data: Specific samurai house (bukeyashiki) addresses; entry fees; best walking routes through the historic district.
improvement_hint: Add body_paragraphs scraped content for Kakunodate to provide cultural context about the samurai houses and their historical families.

reasoning: Query asks about Onomichi. Spots returned include Lemon Valley and Sunset Beach (Setoda, Ikuchijima — technically under Onomichi city but a different island), Okunoshima Poison Gas Museum (Takehara city, not Onomichi), and Hiroshima Station. Municipality code 342050 (Onomichi) is being applied to non-Onomichi locations. Temple Walk, Senkoji Park, Onomichi townscape are absent.
missing_data: Senkoji Park, Onomichi Temple Walk, Onomichi townscape; narrow alley cat street; Shimanami Kaido cycling access.
wrong_data: Okunoshima Museum is in Takehara (342130), not Onomichi; Hiroshima Station is in Hiroshima city — both incorrectly tagged with Onomichi municipality code.
improvement_hint: Fix municipality code assignment during scrape ingestion; verify that coordinates fall within the stated municipality boundary before assigning municipality_code.

reasoning: Nachi Falls leads (prominence 0.95, Natural Monument, Place of Scenic Beauty) with correct Wakayama coordinates. Nachi Primeval Forest, 48 Waterfalls, Mount Nachi, and Nachi Park also present. Noise at tail end (Yamagata STAY YAMAGATA spot) but core data is strong and well-ranked.
missing_data: Kumano Nachi Taisha shrine connection; Nachi Fire Festival (July 14); access from Kii-Katsuura station.

reasoning: Shiretoko (UNESCO WH), Shiretoko Peninsula (prominence 0.9, UNESCO WH), National Park, Mt. Io, World Heritage Center all present with accurate Hokkaido coordinates. Noise at tail (Chinese ryokan in Iwate). Core data excellent for a UNESCO World Heritage nature query.
missing_data: Brown bear wildlife viewing info; restricted access zone (Shiretoko Shiretokozan) details; seasonal access (May-November).

reasoning: Three Mountains of Dewa (prominence 0.9) is first result. Dewa Sanzan Shrine and Dewa Shrine appear further down. reasoning: Yakushima island leads (prominence 1.0, UNESCO WH, Ramsar). National Park, World Heritage Center, history museum, shrine all present with accurate coordinates. Two heritage designation QIDs unresolved ('Q137572758', 'Q106611640'). Good coverage for a major island destination.
missing_data: Jomon Sugi cedar access trail information; sea turtle nesting beaches (Nagata Hama appears but truncated); permit/access restrictions.

reasoning: Query asks about Sado Island. Tool fetched Niigata Japan Heritage and returned Shinano River Flame-Style Pottery and Tokamachi snow country stories — neither covers Sado Island. Sado has its own Japan Heritage story (Sado gold mine etc.) and should be retrievable. Complete intent miss.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional onsen ryokan in Tohoku. Tool returns Akita hotels: ANA Crowne Plaza (urban business hotel), guest houses, an apartment, and a forestry exchange center. reasoning: Query asks specifically for shukubo (temple lodging) on the Shikoku pilgrimage. Tool returns Tokushima hotels with some ryokan types. reasoning: Query asks for farm experience activities in Hokkaido. get_spots returns top tourist landmarks: Matsumae Castle, Goryokaku fortress, Showa-shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Asahi Bridge. These are historical/heritage attractions with zero relation to farm experiences. Complete retrieval failure.

reasoning: Imari-Arita Ware and Karatsu Ware — both Saga pottery traditions — appear with rich descriptions, production areas, and official METI designations. Hakata Ori (weaving) is slightly off-topic but from Fukuoka/Saga area. Query about 'seeing pottery being made' not fully served (no kiln visit locations), but craft identification is excellent.
missing_data: Specific kiln/workshop visit locations in Arita and Karatsu; open studio events; Arita Pottery Fair dates.

reasoning: Query asks about Tohoku regional festivals. Tool returns only Aomori: Hachinohe Sansha Taisai and Aomori Nebuta — both accurate and relevant. National heritage adds Dainichido Bugaku (Akita). However, major Tohoku festivals from Iwate (Sansa Odori), Miyagi (Sendai Tanabata), Akita (Kanto Festival), Yamagata (Hanagasa Festival) are absent. Single-prefecture coverage for a regional query.
missing_data: Akita Kanto Festival, Sendai Tanabata, Yamagata Hanagasa Matsuri, Morioka Sansa Odori — all major Tohoku festivals.
